mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending economic slowdown that erodes consumer spending power and corporate revenue expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating higher corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns do not differ significantly from periods where only one series is strong.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in consumer prices and the federal funds rate indicate an aggressive monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced liquidity, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite simultaneous CPI and rate increases, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes directly forecast near-term corporate revenue growth, leading to immediate positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth shows no correlation with subsequent 5-day equity returns, or earnings revisions move counter to sales momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial production below its long-term trend indicates supply chain contractions and weakening business investment, which suppresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines while equity valuations expand due to monetary accommodation, or the signal fails to predict returns in recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a sustainable labor market that supports consumer-driven corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative forward returns when payrolls rise and unemployment stabilizes, or the interaction effect disappears in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth accelerates while equity returns turn negative due to valuation compression, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a 10-year backtest.
